imn: Yesu guinh sin buatc wuov dauh m'sieqv dorn ziouc gorngv, “M'dauh m'sieqv dorn aah! Gunv bungx hnyouv aqv. Weic zuqc meih sienx yie, meih nyei baengc longx aqv.” Dongh wuov norm ziangh hoc, baengc ziouc longx mi'aqv.

eng: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, calling out, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”
imn: Yesu cuotv wuov norm dorngx mi'aqv. Gan jienv jauv mingh gau, maaih i dauh m'zing maengh nyei mienh gan jienv mingh, heuc jienv gorngv, “Ndaawitv nyei Zeiv-Fun aah! Korv-lienh yie mbuo maah!”

eng: When he had gone indoors, the blind men came to him, and he asked them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?”
imn: Yesu bieqc biauv mingh, wuov deix i dauh m'zing maengh nyei mienh daaih lorz ninh. Yesu naaic gaax ninh mbuo, “Meih mbuo sienx yie haih zorc meih mbuo nyei m'zing fai?”

eng: But the Pharisees said, “It is by the prince of demons that he drives out demons.”
imn: Mv baac Faa^li^si Mienh gorngv, “Mienv nyei hungh bun ninh maaih lingc zunc mienv cuotv hnangv.”

eng: “Whoever acknowledges me before others, I will also acknowledge before my Father in heaven. But whoever disowns me before others, I will disown before my Father in heaven.
imn: “Haaix dauh dorng zuangx nyiemc yie, yie yiem yie nyei tin-dorngh Diex nyei nza'hmien yaac oix nyiemc wuov dauh. Mv baac haaix dauh dorng zuangx maiv nyiemc yie, yie yiem yie nyei tin-dorngh Diex nyei nza'hmien yaac maiv nyiemc wuov dauh.

eng: “Do not suppose that I have come to bring peace to the earth. I did not come to bring peace, but a sword. For I have come to turn
imn: “Maiv dungx hnamv yie daaih weic bun baamh gen taaix-baengh. Yie maiv zeiz daaih weic bun baamh gen taaix-baengh, yie dorh nzuqc ndaauv daaih. Yie daaih weic bun

eng: “ ‘a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law— a man’s enemies will be the members of his own household.’
imn: “+‘dorn caux die maiv horpc, sieqv caux maa maiv horpc, mbuangz caux maa-buoc maiv horpc. Biauv zong mienh ziouc benx ganh nyei win-wangv aqv.’

eng: “To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:
imn: “Yie oix zuqc longc haaix nyungc beiv ih jaax mienh? Ninh mbuo se hnangv fu'jueiv zueiz jienv hei-horngc, naaiv guanh heuc wuov guanh. Ninh mbuo gorngv,

eng: “ ‘We played the pipe for you,
imn: “+‘Yie mbuo biomv hlauv-ndongh nux,

eng: “All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
imn: “Aa Die zorqv nyungc-nyungc jiu bun yie. Cuotv liuz Die, maiv maaih haaix dauh hiuv duqv Dorn. Cuotv liuz Dorn, caux Dorn bun hiuv wuov deix, maiv maaih haaix dauh hiuv duqv Die.”

eng: Then he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out and it was completely restored, just as sound as the other. But the Pharisees went out and plotted how they might kill Jesus.
imn: Yesu ziouc gorngv mbuox buoz waaic wuov dauh mienh, “Sung meih nyei buoz cuotv daaih maah!” Ninh ziouc sung buoz cuotv daaih. Ninh nyei buoz ziouc longx nzengc hnangv longx wuov jieqv nor. Faa^li^si Mienh ziouc cuotv mingh caangh laangh hnangv haaix nor haih daix Yesu.

eng: the one I love, in whom I delight; I will put my Spirit on him, and he will proclaim justice to the nations. He will not quarrel or cry out;
imn: “Mangc maah! Naaiv se yie nyei bou, dongh yie ginv daaih wuov dauh. Yie hnamv haic ninh. Ninh yaac horpc yie nyei hnyouv haic. Yie oix zorqv yie nyei Singx Lingh ceix bun ninh. Ninh oix zunh yie zoux baengh fim nyei jauv bun maanc fingx muangx. Ninh maiv nzaeng jaax yaac maiv heuc qiex mbui,

eng: no one will hear his voice in the streets. A bruised reed he will not break,
imn: yaac maiv maaih haaix dauh haiz ninh yiem jaai-horngc nyei qiex. Luoqc diqc guaengv dopv mingh, ninh yaac maiv aauv ndutv,

eng: and a smoldering wick he will not snuff out, till he has brought justice through to victory. In his name the nations will put their hope.”
imn: dang-cov ganx nyei sioux ninh yaac maiv biomv daic. Ninh oix hnangv naaiv zoux jienv mingh taux baengh fim nyei jauv hingh. Maanc fingx mienh yaac oix sienx kaux ninh

eng: Then they brought him a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute, and Jesus healed him, so that he could both talk and see. All the people were astonished and said, “Could this be the Son of David?”
imn: ziouc maaih lamh hnamv dorngx aqv.” Wuov zanc mienh dorh dauh mienh daaih lorz Yesu. Naaiv dauh mienh zuqc mienv muoqv ninh m'zing maengh yaac muoqv ninh hngongx, maiv haih gorngv waac. Yesu ziouc zorc longx daaih, haih gorngv waac aqv. M'zing yaac haih mangc duqv buatc aqv. Zuangx mienh mbuoqc horngh haic Yesu zoux nyei sic. Laanh naaic laanh, “Naaiv dauh gengh Ndaawitv Hungh nyei Zeiv-Fun fai ni?”

eng: But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, “It is only by Beelzebul, the prince of demons, that this fellow drives out demons.”
imn: Faa^li^si Mienh haiz naaiv deix sic, ninh mbuo gorngv, “Naaiv laanh mienh haih zunc mienv cuotv se laaix ninh longc mienv nyei hungh, Mbe^en^se^mbun , nyei lingc zunc hnangv.”

eng: “Or again, how can anyone enter a strong man’s house and carry off his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can plunder his house.
imn: “Hnangv haaix nor haih bieqc mienh henv mienh nyei biauv caangv ninh nyei ga'naaiv? Oix zuqc ndoh jienv wuov dauh mienh henv mienh ndaangc cingx daaih haih caangv duqv ninh nyei ga'naaiv.

eng: Then some of the Pharisees and teachers of the law said to him, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from you.”
imn: Wuov zanc maaih deix njaaux leiz nyei fin-saeng caux Faa^li^si Mienh gorngv mbuox Yesu, “Fin-saeng aac, yie mbuo oix buatc norm meih bun daaih nyei jangx-hoc.”

eng: While Jesus was still talking to the crowd, his mother and brothers stood outside, wanting to speak to him. Someone told him, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to you.”
imn: Yesu corc caux zuangx mienh gorngv jienv waac wuov zanc, Yesu nyei maa caux ninh nyei youz mbuo taux aqv, souv ga'nyiec maengx oix caux Yesu gorngv waac. Maaih dauh yiem wuov nyei mienh ziouc mbuox Yesu, “Meih nyei maa caux meih nyei youz mbuo souv jienv ga'nyiec maengx, oix caux meih gorngv waac.”

eng: The disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you speak to the people in parables?”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo daaih naaic Yesu, “Sai-Diex weic haaix diuc longc waac-beiv njaaux naaiv deix mienh?”

eng: “Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand. In them is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah:
imn: ‘Ninh mbuo mangc yaac mangc maiv buatc, muangx yaac muangx maiv haiz, yaac maiv mengh baeqc.’ Ninh mbuo doix-diuc nzengc I^saa^yaa douc nyei waac. Ninh gorngv,

eng: “ ‘You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving. For this people’s heart has become calloused;
imn: ‘Meih mbuo ndongc haaix muangx, meih mbuo maiv haih bieqc hnyouv, ndongc haaix mangc, yaac maiv maiv mengh baeqc. Weic zuqc deix baeqc fingx nyei hnyouv nzotv,

eng: “The owner’s servants came to him and said, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?’
imn: “Bou ziouc mingh naaic ndeic-ziouv, ‘Gorx aac, meih haaz mbiauh wuov ndeic maiv zeiz? Hnangv naaic, wuov nyungc laengc mienh nyei mbiauh yiem haaix daaih?’

eng: “ ‘An enemy did this,’ he replied.
imn: “Ndeic-ziouv dau, ‘Naaiv se win-wangv zoux nyei gong.’

eng: He told them still another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed into about sixty pounds of flour until it worked all through the dough.”
imn: Yesu aengx gorngv yietc diuh waac-beiv bun ninh mbuo muangx. “Tin-Hungh zoux Ziouv gunv mienh se hnangv bingv nor. Maaih dauh m'sieqv dorn zorqv deix bingv qouv buo tongv mbuonv. Naaiv deix mbuonv ziouc butv nzengc mi'aqv.”

eng: Jesus spoke all these things to the crowd in parables; he did not say anything to them without using a parable. So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet:
imn: Naaiv deix jauv yietc zungv, Yesu kungx longc waac-beiv njaaux zuangx mienh hnangv. Cuotv liuz longc waac-beiv ninh maiv njaaux haaix nyungc ninh mbuo. Ninh hnangv naaiv zoux weic bun doix nzengc zinh ndaangc douc waac mienh gorngv nyei waac. Ninh gorngv,

eng: “The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again, and then in his joy went and sold all he had and bought that field.
imn: “Tin-Hungh zoux Ziouv gunv mienh, se hnangv jiem nyaanh biopv jienv wuov ndeic nor. Maaih dauh mienh buatc liuz, ninh ziouc aengx biopv jienv aqv. Ninh a'hneiv haic ziouc nzuonx mingh zorqv ninh nyei jaa-dingh nyungc-nyungc ga'naaiv maaic nzengc, dorh nyaanh mingh maaiz wuov nzong ndeic aqv.

eng: “Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant looking for fine pearls. When he found one of great value, he went away and sold everything he had and bought it.
imn: “Tin-Hungh zoux Ziouv gunv mienh yaac hnangv dauh saeng-eix mienh mingh lorz jaaix nyei la'bieiv-zou. Lorz duqv norm gauh jaaix jiex, ninh ziouc nzuonx mingh maaic nzengc ninh maaih nyei yietc zungv aengx maaiz wuov norm la'bieiv-zou.

eng: “Have you understood all these things?” Jesus asked.
imn: Yesu naaic sai-gorx mbuo, “Naaiv deix waac yietc zungv meih mbuo mengh baeqc nyei saah?”

eng: But Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor except in his own town and in his own home.” And he did not do many miracles there because of their lack of faith.
imn: Yesu mbuox ninh mbuo, “Douc waac mienh mingh taux haaix, maaih mienh taaih. Kungx ganh nyei buonv-mungv caux biauv zong mienh maiv taaih hnangv.” Laaix ninh mbuo maiv sienx, yiem wuov Yesu ziouc maiv zoux mbuoqc horngh nyei sic camv aqv.

eng: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the reports about Jesus, and he said to his attendants, “This is John the Baptist; he has risen from the dead! That is why miraculous powers are at work in him.”
imn: Wuov zanc gunv Gaa^li^li Saengv wuov dauh Helotv Hungh muangx haiz Yesu nyei jauv-louc ziouc mbuox ninh nyei sin-hlen mienh, “Naaiv dauh ndongc haaix zungv dongh tengx mienh jiex wuom nyei leiz wuov dauh Yo^han aengx nangh daaih aqv, ninh cingx daaih maaih naaiv nyungc qaqv zoux mbuoqc horngh nyei sic.”

eng: Jesus replied, “They do not need to go away. You give them something to eat.”
imn: Yesu dau, “Ninh mbuo maiv zuqc mingh lorqc. Meih mbuo ganh lorz bun ninh mbuo nyanc maah!”

eng: “We have here only five loaves of bread and two fish,” they answered.
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo gorngv, “Yiem naaiv yie mbuo kungx maaih biaa norm njuov caux i dauh mbiauz hnangv.”

eng: Shortly before dawn Jesus went out to them, walking on the lake. When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified. “It’s a ghost,” they said, and cried out in fear.
imn: Lungh aav lamh njang, buo norm taux juqv norm ziangh hoc mbu'ndongx, Yesu yangh wuom-minc mingh sai-gorx mbuo wuov. Sai-gorx mbuo buatc ninh yangh wuom-minc daaih, ninh mbuo ziouc gamh nziex! haic. Ninh mbuo gorngv, “Aav, naaiv janx-daic njoiz saah!” ziouc haeqv ninh mbuo waiz-waiz nyei heuc.

eng: But Jesus immediately said to them: “Take courage! It is I. Don’t be afraid.”
imn: Yesu liemh zeih gorngv mbuox ninh mbuo, “Gunv bungx hnyouv maah! Se dongh yie, maiv dungx gamh nziex.”

eng: “Lord, if it’s you,” Peter replied, “tell me to come to you on the water.”
imn: Bide ziouc gorngv, “Ziouv aah! Se gorngv zeiz meih nor, heuc yie yangh wuom-minc mingh meih naaic.”

eng: “Come,” he said.
imn: Yesu gorngv, “Daaih maah!”

eng: Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water and came toward Jesus. But when he saw the wind, he was afraid and, beginning to sink, cried out, “Lord, save me!”
imn: Bide ziouc cuotv nzangv yangh wuom-minc hungx jienv Yesu mingh. Mv baac Bide buatc nziaaux hlo, haeqv ninh gamh nziex haic ziouc zemh jienv njiec. Ninh heuc, “Ziouv aah! Ziouv! Njoux yie maah!”

eng: Immediately Jesus reached out his hand and caught him. “You of little faith,” he said, “why did you doubt?”
imn: Yesu liemh zeih sung buoz nanv jienv ninh yaac gorngv, “Meih sienx duqv hei haic. Meih nyei hnyouv weic haaix diuc mbungh mbienv?”

eng: And when they climbed into the boat, the wind died down. Then those who were in the boat worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”
imn: Ninh mbuo i dauh bieqc nzangv. Nziaaux ziang naaic dingh mi'aqv. Yiem nzangv wuov deix ziouc baaix Yesu. Ninh mbuo gorngv, “Ziouv aah! Meih za'gengh Tin-Hungh nyei Dorn aqv.”

eng: Then some Pharisees and teachers of the law came to Jesus from Jerusalem and asked, “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They don’t wash their hands before they eat!”
imn: Wuov zanc, maaih deix Faa^li^si Mienh caux njaaux leiz nyei fin-saeng yiem Ye^lu^saa^lem Zingh daaih lorz Yesu ziouc naaic, “Meih nyei sai-gorx mbuo weic haaix diuc maiv gan mbuo nyei ong-taaix nyei ei-douh? Ninh mbuo maiv ei jienv leiz nzaaux buoz ndaangc cingx daaih nyanc hnaangx.”

eng: “ ‘These people honor me with their lips,
imn: ‘Naaiv deix mienh longc nzuih taaih yie,

eng: but their hearts are far from me. They worship me in vain;
imn: mv baac ninh mbuo nyei hnyouv leih duqv yie go haic. Ninh mbuo zaangc yie se maiv lamh longc.

eng: Then the disciples came to him and asked, “Do you know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this?”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo ziouc daaih mbuox Yesu, “Sai-Diex hiuv nyei fai? Sai-Diex gorngv wuov joux waac Faa^li^si Mienh mbuoqc maiv duqv.”

eng: He replied, “Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be pulled up by the roots. Leave them; they are blind guides. If the blind lead the blind, both will fall into a pit.”
imn: Yesu dau, “Mouz nyungc ga'naaiv maiv zeiz yie nyei tin-dorngh Diex zuangx nyei nor, zungv oix zuqc baeng cuotv nzengc. Sueih binc ninh mbuo aqv. Ninh mbuo zoux dorh jauv nyei mienh mv baac m'zing maengh nyei. Se gorngv m'zing maengh nyei mienh ken m'zing maengh nyei mienh, i dauh zungv zuqc ndortv kuotv aqv.”

eng: Peter said, “Explain the parable to us.”
imn: Bide gorngv, “Tov Sai-Diex porv mengh naaiv diuh waac-beiv bun yie mbuo hiuv oc.”

eng: Jesus did not answer a word. So his disciples came to him and urged him, “Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us.”
imn: Mv baac Yesu maiv dau yietc joux ninh. Sai-gorx mbuo ziouc daaih tov Yesu, “Zunc ninh biaux nqoi maah! Ninh heuc jienv gan jienv mbuo daaih.”

eng: He answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of Israel.”
imn: Yesu dau, “Tin-Hungh paaiv yie daaih lorz I^saa^laa^en Mienh hnangv. Ninh mbuo se hnangv dingx laaih nyei ba'gi yungh nor.”

eng: The woman came and knelt before him. “Lord, help me!” she said.
imn: Naaiv dauh m'sieqv dorn ziouc gueic njiec Yesu nyei nza'hmien yaac gorngv, “Domh Gorx aah! Tov tengx yie maah!”

eng: He replied, “It is not right to take the children’s bread and toss it to the dogs.”
imn: Yesu dau, “Zorqv fu'jueiv nyei hnaangx zoi bun juv nyanc se maiv horpc.”

eng: “Yes it is, Lord,” she said. “Even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their master’s table.”
imn: Wuov dauh Janx-muangz dau, “Domh Gorx aac, zeiz nyei. Mv baac liemh juv m'daaih yiem ninh mbuo nyei ziouv nyei dieh ndiev nzauz hnaangx-hmeiv-ndortv nyanc.”

eng: Then Jesus said to her, “Woman, you have great faith! Your request is granted.” And her daughter was healed at that moment.
imn: Yesu ziouc dau ninh, “Meih naaiv dauh dorc za'gengh sienx duqv longx haic. Ei meih oix longc nyei, meih ziouc duqv aqv.” Wuov dauh Janx-muangz nyei sieqv doix wuov norm ziangh hoc ziouc longx mi'aqv.

eng: His disciples answered, “Where could we get enough bread in this remote place to feed such a crowd?”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo gorngv, “Yiem naaiv deic-bung-huaang yie mbuo hnangv haaix nor haih lorz duqv ga'naaiv gaux uix ndongc naaic camv nyei mienh beuv?”

eng: “How many loaves do you have?” Jesus asked.
imn: Yesu naaic ninh mbuo, “Meih mbuo maaih mbu'ziex norm njuov?”

eng: When they went across the lake, the disciples forgot to take bread. “Be careful,” Jesus said to them. “Be on your guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo jiex wuov ngaanc ziqc koiv-dorn ninh mbuo la'kuqv, maiv dorh njuov mingh. Yesu mbuox ninh mbuo, “Oix zuqc faix fim, mbungh jienv Faa^li^si Mienh caux Saa^ndu^si Mienh nyei bingv .”

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, “It is because we didn’t bring any bread.”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo laanh caux laanh gorngv, “Sai-Diex hnangv naaiv nor gorngv se laaix mbuo maiv dorh njuov daaih.”

eng: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, “Who do people say the Son of Man is?”
imn: Yesu aengx mingh Si^saa^li^yaa Fi^lipv^poi Zingh nyei deic-jaaix. Yiem wuov Yesu naaic ninh nyei sai-gorx, “Mienh gorngv yie, Baamh Mienh nyei Dorn, dongh haaix dauh?”

eng: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Maaih deix gorngv meih dongh tengx mienh jiex wuom nyei leiz wuov dauh Yo^han. Maaih deix gorngv, E^li^yaa . Aengx maaih deix gorngv Ye^le^mi fai yietc dauh douc waac mienh.”

eng: “But what about you?” he asked. “Who do you say I am?”
imn: Yesu aengx naaic ninh mbuo, “Meih mbuo ni? Meih mbuo gorngv yie dongh haaix dauh?”

eng: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
imn: Simon Bide dau, “Meih dongh Giduc , ziangh jienv wuov dauh Tin-Hungh nyei Dorn.”

eng: Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him. “Never, Lord!” he said. “This shall never happen to you!”
imn: Bide ziouc ken Yesu mingh pien deix kuinx ninh, “Ziouv aah! Tov Tin-Hungh cin-maanc maiv dungx bun naaiv deix sic cuotv. Meih yietc liuz maiv zuqc buangh naaiv deix sic.”

eng: Jesus turned and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me; you do not have in mind the concerns of God, but merely human concerns.”
imn: Yesu huin nzuonx gorngv Bide, “Saadaan aah! Biaux nqoi maah! Meih kungx laanh jienv yie hnangv. Meih hnamv nyei sic se maiv zeiz hnangv Tin-Hungh hnamv. Se hnangv baamh mienh hnamv hnangv.”

eng: While he was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased. Listen to him!”
imn: Bide corc gorngv jienv waac wuov zanc, maaih dorv mbuonx njang nyei daaih om jienv ninh mbuo. Aengx maaih qiex yiem wuov mbuonx gorngv, “Naaiv se yie nyei Dorn-hnamv. Yie a'hneiv haic ninh. Muangx ninh maah!”

eng: When the disciples heard this, they fell facedown to the ground, terrified. But Jesus came and touched them. “Get up,” he said. “Don’t be afraid.” When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo haiz naaiv deix qiex, ninh mbuo za'gengh! gamh nziex haic, ziouc mbaapv njiec ndau mingh. Yesu mingh hluo ninh mbuo yaac gorngv, “Jiez sin daaih, maiv dungx gamh nziex.” Ninh mbuo ziouc cau hmien mangc, maiv buatc haaix dauh. Kungx buatc Yesu yiem wuov hnangv.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus instructed them, “Don’t tell anyone what you have seen, until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.”
imn: Yiem mbong njiec wuov zanc, Yesu naeqc waac sai-gorx mbuo. Ninh gorngv, “Cin-maanc maiv dungx gorngv naaiv deix hinc yaangh nyei sic. Zuov yie, Baamh Mienh nyei Dorn, daic mingh, Tin-Hungh aengx bun yie nangh daaih cingx daaih gorngv.”

eng: The disciples asked him, “Why then do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo naaic Yesu, “Njaaux leiz nyei fin-saeng weic haaix diuc gorngv E^li^yaa oix zuqc daaih ndaangc?”

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and asked, “Why couldn’t we drive it out?”
imn: Nqa'haav sai-gorx mbuo daaih bingx jienv naaic Yesu, “Yie mbuo weic haaix diuc zunc wuov dauh mienv maiv cuotv?”

eng: After Jesus and his disciples arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the two-drachma temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the temple tax?”
imn: Yesu caux ninh nyei sai-gorx mbuo mingh taux Kaa^be^naa^um Mungv wuov zanc, siou zaangc Tin-Hungh nyei biauv nyei nzou-zinh wuov deix mienh daaih naaic Bide, “Meih nyei sai-diex maiv cuotv zaangc Tin-Hungh nyei biauv nyei nzou-zinh fai?”

eng: “Yes, he does,” he replied.
imn: Bide dau, “Cuotv nyei.”

eng: “See that you do not despise one of these little ones. For I tell you that their angels in heaven always see the face of my Father in heaven.
imn: “Oix zuqc faix fim, maiv dungx mangc piex yietc dauh naaiv deix faix nyei fu'jueiv. Yie mbuox meih mbuo, naaiv deix fu'jueiv nyei fin-mienh yiem tin-dorngh, zanc-zanc yiem yie nyei tin-dorngh Diex nyei nza'hmien. Weic zuqc yie, Baamh Mienh nyei Dorn, daaih njoux dingx laaih nyei mienh.

eng: “Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
imn: “Yie mbuox zien meih mbuo, meih mbuo yiem baamh gen ndoh haaix nyungc, yiem wuov tin-dorngh wuov nyungc yaac zuqc ndoh aqv. Meih mbuo yiem baamh gen bungx haaix nyungc, yiem wuov tin-dorngh yaac bungx wuov nyungc aqv.

eng: Then Peter came to Jesus and asked, “Lord, how many times shall I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Up to seven times?”
imn: Bide aengx daaih naaic gaax Yesu, “Ziouv aac, se gorngv yie nyei gorx-youz zanc-zanc dorngc yie, yie oix zuqc guangc mbu'ziex nzunc bun ninh? Taux siec nzunc aqv fai?”

eng: Jesus answered, “I tell you, not seven times, but seventy-seven times.
imn: Yesu dau, “Yie maiv zeiz gorngv guangc siec nzunc hnangv. Oix zuqc guangc cietv ziepc nyei siec nzunc.

eng: “At this the servant fell on his knees before him. ‘Be patient with me,’ he begged, ‘and I will pay back everything.’ The servant’s master took pity on him, canceled the debt and let him go.
imn: “Wuov dauh bou ziouc gueic njiec qaqv tov ninh, ‘Tov ziouv sie yie, zuov yie lorz duqv daaih, yie gengh oix zuqc jaauv taux sung nzengc.’ Wuov dauh bou nyei ziouv korv-lienh haic ninh ziouc guangc ninh nyei zaeqv, bungx ninh nzuonx mi'aqv.

eng: “But when that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him a hundred silver coins. He grabbed him and began to choke him. ‘Pay back what you owe me!’ he demanded.
imn: “Naaic dauh bou cuotv mingh yaac buangh zuqc dauh zoux bou doic. Naaiv dauh bou qiemx ninh i ziex norm zinh hnangv, mv baac ninh liemh zeih goh jienv naaiv dauh nyei jaang yaac gorngv, ‘Gaanv jaauv meih qiemx yie nyei zaeqv maah!’ Wuov dauh zoux bou doic ziouc gueic njiec qaqv tov ninh, ‘Tov meih sie yie, yie gengh jaauv nyei lorqc.’ Mv baac ninh maiv kangv, yaac dorh wuov dauh bou mingh wuonx jienv loh zuov ninh jaauv sung zaeqv.

eng: “This is how my heavenly Father will treat each of you unless you forgive your brother or sister from your heart.”
imn: Yesu aengx gorngv setv mueiz waac. “Se gorngv meih mbuo mouz laanh mienh maiv longc hnyouv guangc gorx-youz zoux dorngc meih mbuo nyei sic, yie nyei tin-dorngh Diex yaac fih hnangv nyei maiv guangc bun meih mbuo.”

eng: When Jesus had finished saying these things, he left Galilee and went into the region of Judea to the other side of the Jordan. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
imn: Yesu gorngv liuz naaiv deix waac ziouc cuotv Gaa^li^li Saengv mingh Yu^ndie Saengv jiex wuov ngaanc ziqc Jor^ndaen Ndaaih. Mienh camv haic gan jienv Yesu mingh, ninh ziouc yiem wuov zorc longx ninh mbuo nyei baengc.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him to test him. They asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any and every reason?”
imn: Maaih deix Faa^li^si Mienh daaih seix Yesu ziouc naaic, “Maiv gunv nqox weic haaix nyungc gorn-baengx leih ninh nyei auv, se horpc mbuo nyei leiz fai?”

eng: “Why then,” they asked, “did Moses command that a man give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?”
imn: Faa^li^si Mienh aengx naaic Yesu, “Wuov nyungc, Mose weic haaix diuc mbuox nqox bun zeiv leih cai sou ninh nyei auv ziouc heuc auv mingh aqv?”

eng: The disciples said to him, “If this is the situation between a husband and wife, it is better not to marry.”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo gorngv mbuox Yesu, “Se gorngv longc auv longc nqox nyei jauv hnangv naaic nor, yietc liuz maiv dungx longc gauh longx.”

eng: Then people brought little children to Jesus for him to place his hands on them and pray for them. But the disciples rebuked them.
imn: Maaih deix mienh dorh fu'jueiv daaih lorz Yesu, oix Yesu maan jienv ninh mbuo, tengx ninh mbuo daux gaux. Mv baac sai-gorx mbuo gorngv-hemx naaiv deix mienh. Yesu gorngv, “Gunv bun fu'jueiv daaih lorz yie, maiv dungx laanh ninh mbuo saa. Weic zuqc haaix dauh longc Tin-Hungh zoux Ziouv gunv ninh, wuov dauh se hnangv naaiv deix fu'jueiv nor.” Yesu maan liuz naaiv deix fu'jueiv ninh ziouc mingh mi'aqv.

eng: Just then a man came up to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to get eternal life?”
imn: Maaih nzunc maaih dauh mienh daaih naaic Yesu, “Fin-Saeng aac, yie horpc zuqc zoux haaix nyungc longx nyei sic cingx duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc?”

eng: “Why do you ask me about what is good?” Jesus replied. “There is only One who is good. If you want to enter life, keep the commandments.”
imn: Yesu dau, “Meih weic haaix diuc naaic yie taux longx nyei sic? Kungx maaih nduqc dauh longx hnangv. Se gorngv meih oix duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc, oix zuqc ei jienv leiz-latc zoux.”

eng: “Which ones?” he inquired.
imn: Wuov dauh naaic, “Haaix deix leiz-latc?”

eng: Jesus replied, “ ‘You shall not murder, you shall not commit adultery, you shall not steal, you shall not give false testimony, honor your father and mother,’ and ‘love your neighbor as yourself.’ ”
imn: Yesu dau, “Maiv dungx daix mienh, maiv dungx hienx auv, maiv dungx zoux zaqc yaac maiv dungx zoux jaav zorng-zengx. Oix zuqc tongx nimc meih nyei diex maac,

eng: “All these I have kept,” the young man said. “What do I still lack?”
imn: yaac hnamv mienh hnangv hnamv ganh nor.” Wuov dauh mienh lunx mienh gorngv, “Naaiv deix yietc zungv nyei leiz yie muangx nzengc aqv. Aengx caa haaix nyungc oix zuqc zoux fai?”

eng: Jesus answered, “If you want to be perfect, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
imn: Yesu mbuox ninh, “Se gorngv meih oix zoux dunh yunh nyei mienh, oix zuqc mingh maaic nzengc meih maaih nyei ga'naaiv, zorqv wuov deix nyaanh bun mienh jomc mienh. Hnangv naaiv meih ziouc maaih jaaix nyei ga'naaiv siou jienv wuov tin-dorngh. Zoux liuz, daaih gan yie oc.”

eng: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he had great wealth.
imn: Wuov dauh mienh lunx mienh haiz naaiv deix waac, ninh ziang naaic nzauh nzauh nyei mingh mi'aqv, weic zuqc ninh maaih jaa-dingh hoz haic.

eng: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished and asked, “Who then can be saved?”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo haiz liuz naaiv deix waac ninh mbuo mbuoqc horngh haic aqv. Ninh mbuo naaic gaax, “Wuov nyungc, haaix dauh haih duqv njoux?”

eng: Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
imn: Yesu mangc jienv ninh mbuo yaac gorngv, “Mienh maiv haih njoux ganh mv baac maanc sic Tin-Hungh haih zoux duqv nyei.”

eng: Peter answered him, “We have left everything to follow you! What then will there be for us?”
imn: Bide ziouc gorngv, “Sai-Diex aah! Yie mbuo guangc nzengc nyungc-nyungc daaih gan meih. Wuov nyungc, yie mbuo haih duqv haaix nyungc?”

eng: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard. He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day and sent them into his vineyard.
imn: “Tin-Hungh zoux Ziouv gunv mienh se hnangv dauh a'ngunc huingx nyei ziouv nor. Lungh ndorm nziouv nyei, ninh cuotv mingh lorz mienh tengx ninh zoux a'ngunc huingx nyei gong. Caux gong-mienh dingc ziangx bun horpc mienh pou-tong cingv yietc hnoi nyei gong-zinh, huingx-ziouv ziouc paaiv ninh mbuo mingh wuov huingx zoux gong.

eng: “ ‘Because no one has hired us,’ they answered.
imn: “Ninh mbuo dau, ‘Weic zuqc maiv maaih mienh cingv yie mbuo zoux gong.’

eng: “So the last will be first, and the first will be last.”
imn: Yesu aengx gorngv setv mueiz waac. “Hnangv naaic, nqa'haav wuov deix oix duqv yiem wuov ndaangc. Yiem wuov ndaangc nyei oix zuqc yiem nqa'haav.”

eng: Then the mother of Zebedee’s sons came to Jesus with her sons and, kneeling down, asked a favor of him.
imn: Wuov zanc Se^mbe^ndi nyei auv dorh ninh nyei i dauh dorn daaih Yesu wuov, gueic njiec baaix, tov yietc nyungc.

eng: “What is it you want?” he asked.
imn: Yesu naaic, “Meih oix longc haaix nyungc?”

eng: The crowd rebuked them and told them to be quiet, but they shouted all the louder, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!”
imn: Zuangx mienh hemx ninh mbuo, mbuox ninh mbuo maiv dungx ko lo. Mv baac ninh mbuo jaa heuc gauh mbui, “Ziouv, Ndaawitv nyei Zeiv-Fun aah! Korv-lienh yie mbuo maah!”

eng: Jesus stopped and called them. “What do you want me to do for you?” he asked.
imn: Yesu ziouc dingh jienv heuc ninh mbuo daaih, naaic ninh mbuo, “Meih mbuo oix yie tengx haaix nyungc meih mbuo?”

eng: “Lord,” they answered, “we want our sight.”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Ziouv aah! Tov bun yie mbuo nyei m'zing haih mangc duqv buatc.” Yesu korv-lienh haic ziouc hluo ninh mbuo nyei m'zing. Ninh mbuo liemh zeih haih mangc duqv buatc, ziouc gan jienv Yesu mingh mi'aqv.

eng: This took place to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet:
imn: Naaiv deix sic cuotv daaih weic bun doix nzengc zinh ndaangc douc waac mienh gorngv nyei waac. Ninh gorngv,

eng: “Say to Daughter Zion,
imn: “Oix zuqc mbuox Si^on Zingh nyei mienh,

eng: “Hosanna to the Son of David!” “Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!” “Hosanna in the highest heaven!” When Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred and asked, “Who is this?”
imn: “Ho^saa^naa! Ceng Ndaawitv nyei Zeiv-Fun. Tov Tin-Hungh ceix fuqv dengv Ziouv nyei mbuox daaih wuov dauh. Ho^saa^naa, ceng yiem hlang jiex nyei dorngx wuov dauh Tin-Hungh.” Yesu bieqc Ye^lu^saa^lem wuov zanc, yietc zingh mienh ziouc huaang jiez nzengc daaih, laanh naaic laanh, “Naaiv dauh mienh dongh haaix dauh orqv?”

eng: The crowds answered, “This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee.”
imn: Zuangx mienh dau, “Naaiv se yiem Gaa^li^li Saengv, Naa^saa^letv Mungv daaih nyei douc waac mienh, Yesu.”

eng: “Do you hear what these children are saying?” they asked him.
imn: Ninh mbuo naaic Yesu, “Naaiv deix fu'jueiv gorngv nyei waac meih haiz nyei fai?”

eng: When the disciples saw this, they were amazed. “How did the fig tree wither so quickly?” they asked.
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo buatc ziouc mbuoqc horngh haic yaac naaic gaax, “Naaiv zungh ngongh nyorx ndiangx hnangv haaix nor haih nqaai ndongc naaic siepv?”

eng: So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.”
imn: Ninh mbuo ziouc dau Yesu, “Yie mbuo maiv hiuv duqv.”

eng: “ ‘I will not,’ he answered, but later he changed his mind and went.
imn: “Dorn-hlo dau, ‘Yie maiv oix mingh!’ Mv baac nqa'haav ninh goiv hnyouv aengx mingh aqv.

eng: “Then the father went to the other son and said the same thing. He answered, ‘I will, sir,’ but he did not go.
imn: “Wuov dauh die aengx mingh fih hnangv nyei mbuox dorn-faix. Dorn-faix dau, ‘Oz, yie mingh nyei.’ Mv baac ninh maiv mingh.

eng: “Which of the two did what his father wanted?”
imn: “Naaiv deix i dauh dorn, haaix dauh ei jienv die nyei waac zoux?”

eng: “Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
imn: Yesu naaic, “Wuov nyungc, huingx-ziouv nzuonx wuov zanc ninh oix hnangv haaix nor zoux bun gouv huingx nyei mienh?”

eng: “He will bring those wretches to a wretched end,” they replied, “and he will rent the vineyard to other tenants, who will give him his share of the crop at harvest time.”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Ninh ndongc haaix zungv oix daix guangc wuov deix waaic haic nyei mienh, aengx bun ganh deix mienh gouv ninh nyei huingx, dongh biouv zuoqc nyei ziangh hoc haih bun ninh nyei buonc biouv wuov nyungc mienh.”

eng: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the Scriptures:
imn: Yesu mbuox ninh mbuo, “Ging-Sou fiev jienv naaiv deix waac meih mbuo maiv doqc jiex fai?

eng: “But when the king came in to see the guests, he noticed a man there who was not wearing wedding clothes. He asked, ‘How did you get in here without wedding clothes, friend?’ The man was speechless.
imn: “Hungh diex bieqc mingh mangc kaeqv mienh ziouc buatc maaih dauh maiv zorng puix mingh yinh nyei lui-houx. Hungh diex naaic, ‘Youz aac, meih maiv zorng puix mingh yinh nyei lui-houx, meih hnangv haaix nor duqv bieqc?’ Wuov dauh mienh yaac maiv maaih waac dau aqv.

eng: “Then the king told the attendants, ‘Tie him hand and foot, and throw him outside, into the darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
imn: “Hungh diex ziouc mbuox bou, ‘Zorqv ninh nyei buoz-zaux ndoh jienv dorh ninh mingh guangc ga'nyiec, hmuangx nyei dorngx, yiem wuov bungx-sing bungx-qiex nyei nyiemv, ngaatc jienv nyaah diev.’+”

eng: “For many are invited, but few are chosen.”
imn: Yesu aengx gorngv setv mueiz waac, “Cingv daaih nyei mienh camv, mv baac ginv daaih nyei mienh zoqc.”

eng: “Caesar’s,” they replied.
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Sisaa nyei.”

eng: Then he said to them, “So give back to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.” When they heard this, they were amazed. So they left him and went away.
imn: Yesu ziouc mbuox ninh mbuo, “Hnangv naaic, haaix nyungc Sisaa Hungh nyei, oix zuqc bun Sisaa Hungh. Haaix nyungc Tin-Hungh nyei, oix zuqc bun Tin-Hungh.” Ninh mbuo haiz liuz naaiv deix waac ziouc mbuoqc horngh haic yaac leih nqoi ninh mingh mi'aqv.

eng: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
imn: Zuangx mienh haiz Yesu hnangv naaiv nor njaaux, ninh mbuo gengh! mbuoqc horngh haic.

eng: Hearing that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, the Pharisees got together. One of them, an expert in the law, tested him with this question: “Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the Law?”
imn: Faa^li^si Mienh haiz gorngv Yesu zoux bun Saa^ndu^si Mienh maiv maaih waac dau, ninh mbuo ziouc gapv zunv daaih. Ninh mbuo nyei guanh maaih dauh njaaux leiz nyei fin-saeng oix nduov Yesu gorngv dorngc waac. Ninh ziouc naaic gaax, “Fin-Saeng aac, yietc zungv Leiz-Latc , haaix diuh gauh longc jienv jiex?”

eng: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them, “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose son is he?”
imn: Faa^li^si Mienh corc yiem zunv wuov zanc, Yesu naaic ninh mbuo, “Meih mbuo hnangv haaix nor hnamv? Giduc zoux haaix dauh nyei zeiv-fun?”

eng: “The son of David,” they replied. He said to them, “How is it then that David, speaking by the Spirit, calls him ‘Lord’? For he says,
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Giduc zoux Ndaawitv Hungh nyei zeiv-fun.” Yesu aengx naaic ninh mbuo, “Wuov nyungc, Singx Lingh weic haaix diuc bun Ndaawitv heuc Giduc zoux ‘Ziouv’? Ndaawitv gorngv,

eng: “ ‘The Lord said to my Lord:
imn: ‘Ziouv gorngv mbuox yie nyei Ziouv,

eng: As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately. “Tell us,” they said, “when will this happen, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
imn: Yesu zueiz jienv Ga'lanv Mbong wuov zanc, sai-gorx mbuo ganh lengc jeiv daaih naaic ninh, “Tov mbuox yie mbuo oc. Naaiv deix sic haaix zanc oix cuotv? Maaih haaix nyungc jangx-hoc oix bun cing meih aengx oix daaih yaac bun cing baamh gen nyei setv mueiz oix taux?”

eng: “Immediately after the distress of those days
imn: “Wuov deix hnoi nyei zeqc naanc yietv jiex liuz,

eng: “At midnight the cry rang out: ‘Here’s the bridegroom! Come out to meet him!’
imn: “Taux ndaamv muonz, maaih qiex heuc jienv gorngv, ‘Siang-laangh taux aav loh! Daaih zipv ninh aqv.’

eng: “Then all the virgins woke up and trimmed their lamps. The foolish ones said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil; our lamps are going out.’
imn: “Wuov deix ziepc dauh sieqv-dorn nyie daaih liuc leiz ninh mbuo nyei dang. Hngongx wuov deix mbuox guai wuov deix, ‘Tov bun deix youh yie mbuo. Yie mbuo nyei dang aav lamh daic aqv.’

eng: “ ‘No,’ they replied, ‘there may not be enough for both us and you. Instead, go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.’
imn: “Guai wuov deix dau, ‘Gamh nziex youh maiv gaux bun meih mbuo caux yie mbuo lomh nzoih longc. Meih mbuo oix zuqc mingh wuov maaic youh nyei dorngx maaiz.’

eng: “But while they were on their way to buy the oil, the bridegroom arrived. The virgins who were ready went in with him to the wedding banquet. And the door was shut.
imn: “Hngongx wuov deix sieqv-dorn ziouc mingh maaiz youh maiv gaengh nzuonx, siang-laangh taux daaih aqv. Liuc leiz ziangx wuov deix biaa dauh ziouc gan jienv siang-laangh bieqc mingh nyanc cing-jaa-hnaangx. Gaengh ziouc guon jienv aqv.

eng: “Later the others also came. ‘Lord, Lord,’ they said, ‘open the door for us!’
imn: “Nqa'haav hngongx wuov deix sieqv-dorn taux daaih ziouc heuc, ‘Domh Gorx aac, Domh Gorx! Koi gaengh bun yie mbuo bieqc oc.’

eng: “But he replied, ‘Truly I tell you, I don’t know you.’
imn: “Siang-laangh dau, ‘Yie gorngv zien nyei, yie maiv hiuv duqv meih mbuo.’+”

eng: “Therefore keep watch, because you do not know the day or the hour.
imn: Yesu aengx gorngv setv mueiz waac, “Hnangv naaic, meih mbuo oix zuqc ging hnyouv mbungh jienv, weic zuqc meih mbuo maiv hiuv duqv wuov norm hnoi, wuov norm ziangh hoc.

eng: “His master replied, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Come and share your master’s happiness!’
imn: “Ninh nyei ziouv gorngv, ‘Longx aqv. Meih se longx haic, hnyouv zingx nyei bou. Deix baav nyaanh hnangv meih goux duqv ndongc naaic longx, yie oix bun meih goux camv nyei. Gunv bieqc daaih caux yie njien-youh maah!’

eng: “The man with two bags of gold also came. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘you entrusted me with two bags of gold; see, I have gained two more.’
imn: “Duqv i waanc zinh wuov dauh bou aengx bieqc daaih gorngv, ‘Ziouv aac, meih bun i waanc zinh yie. Mangc gaax naaiv! Yie aengx zornc duqv i waanc.’ Ninh nyei ziouv gorngv, ‘Longx haic aqv. Meih se longx haic, hnyouv zingx nyei bou. Deix baav nyaanh hnangv meih goux duqv ndongc naaiv longx, yie oix bun meih goux camv. Gunv bieqc daaih caux yie njien-youh aqv.’

eng: “The King will reply, ‘Truly I tell you, whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you did for me.’
imn: “Hungh Diex ziouc dau ninh mbuo, ‘Yie gorngv zien mbuox meih mbuo, meih mbuo zoux bun yietc dauh yie nyei gorx-youz, maiv gunv ninh zoux gauh faix jiex wuov dauh, meih mbuo ziouc zoux bun yie aqv.’

eng: “They also will answer, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or needing clothes or sick or in prison, and did not help you?’
imn: “Ninh mbuo ziouc dau ninh, ‘Ziouv aac, yie mbuo ziangh haaix buatc meih ngorc fai jaang nqaatv, fai zoux ganh fingx mienh fai maiv maaih lui-houx zuqv, fai butv baengc fai zuqc wuonx jienv loh yaac maiv tengx meih?’

eng: “He will reply, ‘Truly I tell you, whatever you did not do for one of the least of these, you did not do for me.’
imn: “Hungh Diex ziouc dau, ‘Yie mbuox zien meih mbuo, meih mbuo maiv tengx yietc dauh naaiv deix zoux faix jiex nyei mienh, meih mbuo yaac maiv tengx yie.’+”

eng: “Then they will go away to eternal punishment, but the righteous to eternal life.”
imn: Yesu aengx gorngv setv mueiz waac, “Hnangv naaic, naaiv deix oix zuqc cuotv mingh yietc liuz siouc zuiz. Mv baac kuv mienh wuov deix ziouc duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc.”

eng: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, he said to his disciples, “As you know, the Passover is two days away—and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.”
imn: Yesu gorngv liuz naaiv deix yietc zungv waac, ninh mbuox ninh nyei sai-gorx, “Meih mbuo hiuv duqv maaih i hnoi hnangv taux Siex Maengc Zipv aqv. Yie, Baamh Mienh nyei Dorn, ziouc zuqc zorqv jiu bun mienh ding ziepc nzaangc jaax.”

eng: While Jesus was in Bethany in the home of Simon the Leper, a woman came to him with an alabaster jar of very expensive perfume, which she poured on his head as he was reclining at the table.
imn: Yesu yiem Mbe^taa^ni Laangz, butv jiex gomh huv baengc wuov dauh Simon nyei biauv. Nyanc jienv hnaangx nyei ziangh hoc, maaih dauh m'sieqv dorn dorh norm la'bieiv-baengh dapv buangv jaaix haic nyei youh ndaang daaih dox Yesu nyei m'nqorngv. Sai-gorx mbuo buatc hnangv naaic zoux, ninh mbuo qiex jiez haic ziouc gorngv, “Baeqc baeqc zaaux guangc zoux haaix nyungc? Naaiv deix youh ndaang haih maaic duqv nyaanh camv haic bun mienh jomc mienh niaa.”

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Where do you want us to make preparations for you to eat the Passover?”
imn: Jiex dongh Maiv An Bingv nyei Njuov nyei Zipv daauh hnoi, sai-gorx mbuo daaih naaic Yesu, “Sai-Diex oix yie mbuo yiem haaix mbenc Siex Maengc Zipv nyei hnaangx bun Sai-Diex nyanc?”

eng: When evening came, Jesus was reclining at the table with the Twelve. And while they were eating, he said, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me.”
imn: Lungh maanz-hmuangx, Yesu caux ninh nyei ziepc nyeic dauh sai-gorx faaux dieh nyanc hnaangx. Nyanc jienv hnaangx wuov zanc, Yesu gorngv, “Yie gorngv zien mbuox meih mbuo, yiem meih mbuo mbu'ndongx maaih dauh oix maaic yie.”

eng: They were very sad and began to say to him one after the other, “Surely you don’t mean me, Lord?”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo za'gengh! nzauh haic ziouc yietc dauh yietc dauh nyei naaic Yesu, “Ziouv aac, maiv zeiz yie saah?”

eng: Then Judas, the one who would betray him, said, “Surely you don’t mean me, Rabbi?”
imn: Maaic Yesu wuov dauh Yu^ndaatc gorngv, “Sai-Diex aah! Maiv zeiz yie saah?”

eng: Jesus answered, “You have said so.” While they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke it and gave it to his disciples, saying, “Take and eat; this is my body.”
imn: Yesu dau, “Naaic se meih ganh gorngv aqv.” Nyanc jienv wuov zanc, Yesu zorqv njuov daaih, laengz zingh liuz Tin-Hungh, ziouc maeqv nqoi bun ninh nyei sai-gorx mbuo, yaac gorngv, “Zipv daaih nyanc aqv. Naaiv se yie nyei sin.”

eng: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
imn: Ninh mbuo baaux liuz nzung, ziouc cuotv daaih, mingh wuov Ga'lanv Mbong mi'aqv.

eng: Then Jesus told them, “This very night you will all fall away on account of me, for it is written:
imn: Wuov zanc Yesu gorngv mbuox ninh nyei sai-gorx, “Ih muonz meih mbuo yietc zungv oix guangc jienv yie biaux, weic zuqc Ging-Sou fiev jienv gorngv,

eng: “ ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’ But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee.”
imn: ‘Tin-Hungh oix daix ba'gi yungh nyei ziouv. Ba'gi yungh guanh ziouc nzaanx nzengc mi'aqv.’ Mv baac yie daic mingh, Tin-Hungh aengx bun yie nangh daaih, yie ziouc mingh Gaa^li^li Saengv ndaangc meih mbuo.”

eng: Peter replied, “Even if all fall away on account of you, I never will.”
imn: Bide gorngv mbuox Yesu, “Maiv gunv ninh mbuo yietc zungv guangc Sai-Diex, yie yietc liuz maiv guangc.”

eng: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus answered, “this very night, before the rooster crows, you will disown me three times.”
imn: Yesu gorngv mbuox Bide, “Yie gorngv zien mbuox meih. Ih muonz, jai zungv maiv gaengh nqaaix, meih zungv maiv nyiemc yie buo nzunc.”

eng: But Peter declared, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never disown you.” And all the other disciples said the same.
imn: Bide gorngv, “Maiv gunv yie zuqc caux Sai-Diex daic, yie yietc liuz nyiemc, maiv guangc meih.” Wuov deix sai-gorx yietc zungv yaac fih hnangv nyei gorngv.

eng: Going a little farther, he fell with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, may this cup be taken from me. Yet not as I will, but as you will.”
imn: Yesu aengx jiex mingh deix ziouc mbaapv njiec, hmien taux ndau daux gaux gorngv, “Yie nyei Die aah! Se gorngv horpc nyei nor, tov Die zorqv nqoi yie oix zuqc hopv naaiv norm zaanv. Mv baac maiv dungx ei yie nyei eix. Oix zuqc ei Die nyei eix hnangv.”

eng: He went away a second time and prayed, “My Father, if it is not possible for this cup to be taken away unless I drink it, may your will be done.”
imn: Yesu aengx mingh daux gaux da'nyeic nzunc. Ninh gorngv, “Yie nyei Die aah! Se gorngv naaiv norm zaanv yie simv maiv ndutv, za'gengh oix zuqc hopv nor, tov ei Die nyei eix zoux ziangx.” Yesu aengx nzuonx taux sai-gorx mbuo wuov, buatc ninh mbuo m'njormh mi'aqv, weic zuqc m'zing nyuix haic, nungx maiv nqoi.

eng: Jesus replied, “Do what you came for, friend.”
imn: Yesu gorngv, “Youz aac, meih oix zoux haaix nyungc gaanv zoux siepv aqv.”

eng: Then the men stepped forward, seized Jesus and arrested him. With that, one of Jesus’ companions reached for his sword, drew it out and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
imn: Wuov deix mienh ziouc daaih njiec buoz zorqv jienv Yesu. Maaih dauh caux Yesu yiem wuov nyei mienh baeng ninh nyei nzuqc ndaauv cuotv, hngaqv domh sai mienh nyei nouh, pai zuqc nouh nyei yietc bung m'normh ndutv mingh. Yesu mbuox ninh mbuo, “Zorqv meih nyei nzuqc ndaauv tapv jienv paiv maah! Haaix dauh longc nzuqc ndaauv, zungv oix zuqc laaix nzuqc ndaauv daic. Meih maiv hiuv fai? Yie haih tov yie nyei Die, nduqc dangh hnangv ninh ziouc paaiv fin-mienh baeng gauh camv ziepc nyeic guanh daaih tengx yie. Mv baac, se gorngv yie hnangv naaic zoux, sic hnangv haaix nor haih cuotv daaih doix Ging-Sou fiev horpc zuqc cuotv nyei waac?”

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for false evidence against Jesus so that they could put him to death. But they did not find any, though many false witnesses came forward.
imn: Sai mienh bieiv caux Gunv Sic Wuic nyei zuangx mienh lorz jaav zorng-zengx gox Yesu weic bun ninh zuqc daix. Mv baac maiv gunv maaih mienh camv daaih zoux jaav zorng-zengx, yaac lorz maiv duqv haaix nyungc gorn-baengx gox ninh. Zoux gau, maaih i dauh cuotv daaih gorngv, “Naaiv laanh mienh gorngv, ‘Yie haih caeqv nzengc zaangc Tin-Hungh nyei biauv. Buo hnoi gu'nyuoz aengx ganh ceix jiez daaih.’+”

eng: Then the high priest stood up and said to Jesus, “Are you not going to answer? What is this testimony that these men are bringing against you?” But Jesus remained silent.
imn: Domh sai mienh ziouc souv jiez daaih naaic Yesu, “Naaiv deix mienh hnangv naaiv nor gox meih, meih maiv dau fai?” Mv baac Yesu maiv ko lo. Domh sai mienh aengx mbuox Yesu, “Yie ziangv jienv yietc liuz ziangh nyei Tin-Hungh hatc meih gorngv zaqc mbuox yie mbuo, meih za'gengh Tin-Hungh nyei Dorn, Giduc, zeiz fai maiv zeiz?”

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, “He has spoken blasphemy! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, now you have heard the blasphemy. What do you think?”
imn: Domh sai mienh haiz naaiv deix waac, ziouc betv ninh ganh nyei lui yaac gorngv, “Ninh ki Tin-Hungh! Mbuo maiv zuqc lorz haaix dauh zoux zorng-zengx aqv. Meih mbuo ih zanc zungv haiz ninh ki Tin-Hungh. Meih mbuo hnangv haaix nor hnamv?”

eng: “He is worthy of death,” they answered. Then they spit in his face and struck him with their fists. Others slapped him and said, “Prophesy to us, Messiah. Who hit you?”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Ninh dorngc aqv. Zic duqv daic aqv.” Ninh mbuo ziouc tuiv wuom-nzuih naetv Yesu nyei hmien yaac longc buoz-juonh mborqv. Maaih deix longc buoz-zaangv mbaix, yaac gorngv, “Meih naaic dauh Giduc aah! Cai gaax! Mbuox yie mbuo maah! Haaix dauh mborqv meih?”

eng: Now Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, and a servant girl came to him. “You also were with Jesus of Galilee,” she said.
imn: Bide zueiz jienv biauv-hlen laatc gu'nyuoz wuov zanc, maaih dauh m'sieqv bou daaih gorngv, “Meih yaac gan wuov dauh Gaa^li^li Mienh, Yesu, maiv zeiz?”

eng: But he denied it before them all. “I don’t know what you’re talking about,” he said.
imn: Mv baac Bide dorng jienv zuangx mienh maiv nyiemc. Ninh gorngv, “Yie maiv hiuv duqv meih gorngv haaix nyungc.”

eng: Then he went out to the gateway, where another servant girl saw him and said to the people there, “This fellow was with Jesus of Nazareth.”
imn: Gorngv liuz, Bide ziouc mingh taux wuov laatc gaengh. Aengx maaih dauh m'sieqv bou buatc ninh ziouc mbuox yiem wuov ga'hlen nyei mienh, “Naaiv dauh mienh yaac gan Naa^saa^letv Mungv nyei Yesu aex.”

eng: He denied it again, with an oath: “I don’t know the man!”
imn: Bide aengx maiv nyiemc. Ninh gorngv, “Ndouv haaix nyungc yaac ndouv duqv, yie maiv hiuv wuov laanh mienh.”

eng: After a little while, those standing there went up to Peter and said, “Surely you are one of them; your accent gives you away.”
imn: Yietc zanc jiex, souv jienv wuov ga'hlen nyei mienh daaih gorngv Bide, “Zeiz nyei. Meih gengh yietc dauh wuov deix mienh aqv. Meih gorngv waac nyei qiex bun meih ganh bingx maiv mbueiz aqv.”

eng: Then he began to call down curses, and he swore to them, “I don’t know the man!”
imn: Bide ziouc aengx ndouv jiez zioux gorngv, “Yie za'gengh! maiv hiuv duqv wuov laanh mienh.”

eng: Immediately a rooster crowed. Then Peter remembered the word Jesus had spoken: “Before the rooster crows, you will disown me three times.” And he went outside and wept bitterly.
imn: Liemh zeih jai nqaaix aqv. Bide jangx zuqc Yesu gorngv nyei waac, “Jai zungv maiv gaengh nqaaix, meih maiv nyiemc yie buo nzunc.” Bide ziouc cuotv mingh nyiemv duqv kouv haic.

eng: Early in the morning, all the chief priests and the elders of the people made their plans how to have Jesus executed. So they bound him, led him away and handed him over to Pilate the governor.
imn: Lungh ndorm nziouv, zuangx sai mienh bieiv caux Yiutai Mienh nyei mienh gox gapv zunv caangh laangh ziangx hnangv haaix nor haih daix Yesu. Ninh mbuo ziouc ndoh Yesu, ken mingh jiu bun Lomaa Zungx, Bilaatv.

eng: “What is that to us?” they replied. “That’s your responsibility.” So Judas threw the money into the temple and left. Then he went away and hanged himself.
imn: Wuov deix mienh dau, “Naaic maaih haaix nyungc paanx taux yie mbuo? Naaiv meih ganh nyei sic aqv.” Yu^ndaatc ziouc zoi wuov deix nyaanh guangc wuov zaangc Tin-Hungh nyei biauv gu'nyuoz cuotv mingh, ziouc mingh ndiux jaang daic mi'aqv.

eng: Meanwhile Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
imn: hnangv Ziouv mbuox yie zoux nor.” Wuov zanc, Yesu yiem Bilaatv Zungx nyei nza'hmien. Bilaatv naaic ninh, “Meih zoux Yiutai Mienh nyei hungh?”

eng: While Pilate was sitting on the judge’s seat, his wife sent him this message: “Don’t have anything to do with that innocent man, for I have suffered a great deal today in a dream because of him.”
imn: Bilaatv zueiz jienv siemv zuiz weic wuov zanc, ninh nyei auv bun waac mingh mbuox ninh, “Maiv dungx zoux haaix nyungc bun wuov laanh kuv mienh weic zuqc a'hmuangx yie mbeix mbeix taux naaiv laanh mienh ziouc haiz hnyouv maiv longx yietc deix.”

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barabbas and to have Jesus executed.
imn: Sai mienh bieiv caux mienh gox nduov zuangx mienh tov Bilaatv bungx Mbaa^lapv^mbatc, daix Yesu guangc. Bilaatv ziouc naaic zuangx mienh, “Naaiv deix i dauh, meih mbuo oix yie bungx haaix dauh?”

eng: “Barabbas,” they answered. “What shall I do, then, with Jesus who is called the Messiah?” Pilate asked.
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Bungx Mbaa^lapv^mbatc.” Bilaatv ziouc naaic, “Wuov nyungc, Yesu, dongh mienh heuc Giduc wuov dauh, meih mbuo oix yie hnangv haaix nor zoux?”

eng: They all answered, “Crucify him!” “Why? What crime has he committed?” asked Pilate.
imn: Ninh mbuo yietc zungv dau, “Zorqv ninh ding jienv ziepc nzaangc jaax!” Bilaatv aengx naaic ninh mbuo, “Wuov nyungc, ninh zoux dorngc haaix nyungc zuiz?”

eng: All the people answered, “His blood is on us and on our children!”
imn: Zuangx mienh dongh joux waac dau, “Naaiv dauh mienh daic, liouc cuotv ninh nyei nziaamv, youh yie mbuo caux yie mbuo ganh nyei fun-faqv ndaam-dorng.”

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. But he had Jesus flogged, and handed him over to be crucified.
imn: Bilaatv ziouc bungx Mbaa^lapv^mbatc bun ninh mbuo. Mv baac Yesu, ninh bun zuqc biaav-bin mborqv liuz, jiu bun baeng dorh mingh ding ziepc nzaangc jaax.

eng: When some of those standing there heard this, they said, “He’s calling Elijah.”
imn: Maaih deix souv jienv wuov nyei mienh haiz ninh gorngv naaiv deix waac ziouc gorngv, “Ov, ninh heuc E^li^yaa.” Maaih dauh yiem wuov nyei mienh liemh zeih tiux mingh zorqv lai-nzeix-sortv ziemx a'ngunc diuv-sui daaih, longc luoqc diqc guaengv baqv jienv cunv bun Yesu hopv. Mv baac maaih deix yiem wuov nyei mienh gorngv, “Mbuo zuov mangc gaax, E^li^yaa gengh daaih njoux ninh nyei fai?”

eng: And when Jesus had cried out again in a loud voice, he gave up his spirit.
imn: Yesu aengx heuc nzunc mbui nyei liuz, ziouc dangx qiex mi'aqv. Yiem zaangc Tin-Hungh nyei biauv gu'nyuoz wuov kuaaiv mbaeqc gaengh ndie yiem gu'nguaaic mbaaix dorng ga'ndiev, zoux i maengx mi'aqv. Ndau yaac dongz, la'bieiv yaac mbaaix nzengc. Janx-daic zouv yaac nqoi mi'aqv. Tin-Hungh nyei cing-nzengc baeqc fingx, dongh daic mingh wuov deix, maaih camv nyei nangh daaih cuotv zouv mingh. Yesu nangh daaih wuov zanc, wuov deix mienh bieqc wuov norm cing-nzengc nyei zingh, Ye^lu^saa^lem. Maaih mienh camv nyei duqv buatc ninh mbuo.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and all that had happened, they were terrified, and exclaimed, “Surely he was the Son of God!”
imn: Zuov Yesu wuov dauh baeng-bieiv caux ninh nyei baeng buatc ndau dongz yaac buatc nyungc-nyungc sic cuotv, haeqv ninh mbuo gamh nziex haic. Ninh mbuo gorngv, “Naaiv dauh za'gengh Tin-Hungh nyei Dorn aqv.”

eng: “Take a guard,” Pilate answered. “Go, make the tomb as secure as you know how.” So they went and made the tomb secure by putting a seal on the stone and posting the guard.
imn: Bilaatv mbuox ninh mbuo, “Wuov nyungc, gunv dorh baeng mingh ei meih mbuo haih zoux nyei longx-longx nyei zuov jienv zouv maah!”
